parallelism.notes: Distribute qualifying blocks evenly across threads using static scheduling to eliminate dynamic scheduling overhead. Each thread gets a contiguous chunk of qualifying blocks for better prefetch behavior.

optimization_notes: Category B+C: The iter_0 inner loop at 22ms/9M rows across 64 cores indicates ~19 cycles/row — poor vectorization due to branching. Three changes: (1) Use branchless SIMD-friendly accumulation — compute all filter predicates as integer masks, combine with bitwise AND, convert to double mask (0.0 or 1.0), multiply into the accumulation expression so the compiler can auto-vectorize with no branch. The inner loop should be: int32_t m = (sd[i]>=DATE_LO) & (sd[i]<DATE_HI) & (disc[i]>=DISC_LO) & (disc[i]<=DISC_HI) & (qty[i]<QTY_THR); local_sum += m * ep[i] * disc[i]. (2) Call madvise(MADV_SEQUENTIAL) on each mmap'd column file before the scan to improve kernel readahead — the unmeasured 33ms gap (55ms total minus 22ms scan) likely includes page fault overhead for touching mmap'd pages; madvise reduces this. (3) Use static scheduling instead of dynamic(1) — with ~90 blocks and 64 threads the dynamic scheduling overhead is non-trivial relative to per-block work (~0.24ms/block). Also add __builtin_prefetch hints to prefetch the next block's data while processing the current block. Compile with -O3 -march=native -ffast-math to enable FMA and AVX vectorization of the branchless loop.
